blocks.html_en: This Report

sentences.en: This Report
sentences.zh: 本报告

blocks.html_en: This report makes five key points. Collectively, they show the association between income and happiness is highly systematic, but that its implications are linear in some contexts yet exponential in others.

sentences.en: This report makes five key points.
sentences.zh: 这份报告提出了五个要点。

sentences.en: Collectively, they show the association between income and happiness is highly systematic, but that its implications are linear in some contexts yet exponential in others.
sentences.zh: 总的来说，他们表明收入和幸福之间的关联是高度系统化的，但其影响在某些情况下是线性的，而在其他情况下是指数级的。

blocks.html_en: As is probably obvious, reasoning linearly in a situation that calls for exponential thinking, or vice versa, could lead to conclusions that are wrong. Not just a little wrong, but very wrong. For example, people sometimes assume that the diminishing value of dollars for happiness means that larger incomes have diminishing value for happiness. In my experience, this is not uncommonly asserted even by experts. While this might sound true given what I’ve said so far, it appears to be false in an important sense: as I’ll show, incomes vary exponentially in size, to a degree that appears to completely offset the declining marginal value of dollars for happiness.

blocks.html_en: Most of this paper is devoted to considering the implications of the association between income and happiness. In other words, what effects would we predict in the real-world if we extrapolate from the shape of the association? And how might our thinking need to change in different contexts? To establish a foundation for this analysis, I first show that the average trend in happiness across income levels fits Bernoulli’s theory with almost perfect precision.

blocks.html_en: This trend builds upon data on money and happiness from trackyourhappiness.org, as reported recently in other research (17–19). This includes ~1.7 million real-time reports of experienced happiness from 33,391 employed, working-age adults (ages 18 to 65) living in the United States. Participants received notifications on their smartphones at randomly-selected times during daily life, and were asked to report on their experiences at the moment just before the notification. Experienced happiness was measured with the question “How do you feel right now?” on a continuous response scale with endpoints labeled “Very bad” and “Very good.” Household income was measured with the question, “What is your total annual household income before taxes?” with answers collected in income bands. Happiness was also measured in evaluative terms via life satisfaction, including a continuous life satisfaction scale, a four-level life satisfaction scale, and the five-item seven-level Satisfaction With Life Scale (20).

blocks.html_en: Results

sentences.en: Results
sentences.zh: 结果

blocks.html_en: Point 1: The association between Log(income) and happiness was almost perfectly linear

sentences.en: Point 1: The association between Log(income) and happiness was almost perfectly linear
sentences.zh: 第一点：Log（收入）和幸福感之间的关联几乎是完美的线性关系

blocks.html_en: The most common way to account for the declining marginal utility of dollars is to log transform income before correlating it with happiness. We will consider the implications of this transformation later. But first let us simply ask: how systematic is the shape of the association between happiness and Log(income)? When I previously reported this association (17) I did not calculate how well (or poorly) a linear model could explain the overall shape of the association. To quantify it, let us calculate the group-level association between Log(income) and the average happiness of the people in each income category. This averages out the substantial variation in individual happiness that income cannot explain, and isolates the underlying shape of the association between income and happiness.

sentences.en: The most common way to account for the declining marginal utility of dollars is to log transform income before correlating it with happiness.
sentences.zh: 解释美元边际效用下降的最常见方法是先记录变换收入，然后再将其与幸福感关联起来。

sentences.en: We will consider the implications of this transformation later.
sentences.zh: 稍后我们将考虑这种转变的影响。

blocks.html_en: Results show that Log(income) and happiness were not merely positively related. From $10,000/y to over $500,000/y, they were correlated at the aggregate level to an extremely high degree, with group-level r’s = 0.98-0.99 across a range of happiness measures, including both experienced happiness and overall life satisfaction, see Fig. 1. These group-level correlations remain high when controlling for demographic variables (age, gender, education level, and marriage), r’s = 0.95-0.99.

blocks.html_en: Fig. 1. Mean levels of happiness, including experienced happiness (real-time feelings) and overall life satisfaction, for each income category. The group-level correlations between happiness and Log(income) is displayed. Income axis is log scaled, and error bars indicate 95% confidence intervals. Because the top 3 income categories have progressively fewer people and progressively wider confidence intervals, a composite point (displayed in gray) that combines them and is comparable in size to the other income categories is also plotted for reference (it is not included in the correlation or fit line calculations).

blocks.html_en: In other words, the average happiness of people in each income category was almost perfectly linearly associated with that category’s Log(income), explaining virtually 100% of group-level variation. This is just as Bernoulli’s nearly 300-year-old theory predicts, if we extend it from wealth to income and from “decision utility” to happiness.

sentences.zh: 换句话说，每个收入类别的人们的平均幸福感几乎与该类别的 Log（收入）完全线性相关，几乎可以 100% 地解释群体层面的差异。

blocks.html_en: When explaining how happiness varies between income levels, a linear prediction from Log(income) was superbly accurate1. If we assume this remarkably systematic shape indicates the “price of happiness”, what are the implications for the rest of human life?

blocks.html_en: Point 2: This entails a diminishing marginal value of dollars, but shouldn’t be confused with a plateau

sentences.en: Point 2: This entails a diminishing marginal value of dollars, but shouldn’t be confused with a plateau
sentences.zh: 第 2 点：这意味着美元的边际价值递减，但不应与平稳期相混淆

blocks.html_en: If happiness rises linearly with Log(income), it implies that the marginal value of a dollar diminishes exponentially, at least in terms of predicting happiness. For example, an extra $10,000 would be expected to “matter” much more to happiness for a person earning $25,000/y than a person earning $200,000/y, and it would be expected to take ever-greater increments of income to achieve the same increment in happiness as incomes rise. When the association between happiness and income is plotted on a linear axis instead of a logarithmic axis, the declining marginal value of a dollar is clear (see Fig. 2).

sentences.en: If happiness rises linearly with Log(income), it implies that the marginal value of a dollar diminishes exponentially, at least in terms of predicting happiness.
sentences.zh: 如果幸福感随 Log（收入）线性上升，则意味着一美元的边际价值呈指数递减，至少在预测幸福感方面是如此。

sentences.en: For example, an extra $10,000 would be expected to “matter” much more to happiness for a person earning $25,000/y than a person earning $200,000/y, and it would be expected to take ever-greater increments of income to achieve the same increment in happiness as incomes rise.

sentences.en: When the association between happiness and income is plotted on a linear axis instead of a logarithmic axis, the declining marginal value of a dollar is clear (see Fig. 2).

blocks.html_en: Fig. 2. Estimated levels of happiness plotted as a linear function of Log(income), which Figure 1 shows fit the data extremely well. Income axis is linear, not log transformed.

sentences.en: Fig. 2.
Estimated levels of happiness plotted as a linear function of Log(income), which Figure 1 shows fit the data extremely well.
sentences.zh: 估计的幸福水平绘制为 Log（收入）的线性函数，图 1 显示与数据非常吻合。

blocks.html_en: The declining marginal value of a dollar is no more or less true when plotted on a linear axis, of course, but plotting it this way makes its truth obvious. The shapes observed in Fig. 1 and Fig. 2 are equivalent to each other and are equally correct. The problem is that Fig. 2 can be misleading. While it might seem that this curve is asymptotic and will quickly reach a maximum value for happiness beyond which additional income makes no difference, a mathematically linear relationship between happiness and Log(income) is, in reality, not asymptotic. It will never reach a plateau. There may well be some income threshold in the real world beyond which happiness fails to increase, but the point to recognize here is that the existence of a plateau is not predicted mathematically by a logarithmic association.

sentences.en: The declining marginal value of a dollar is no more or less true when plotted on a linear axis, of course, but plotting it this way makes its truth obvious.
sentences.zh: 当然，当在线性轴上绘制时，美元边际价值的下降或多或少是真实的，但以这种方式绘制它使其真相显而易见。

sentences.en: The shapes observed in Fig. 1 and Fig. 2 are equivalent to each other and are equally correct.

sentences.en: While it might seem that this curve is asymptotic and will quickly reach a maximum value for happiness beyond which additional income makes no difference, a mathematically linear relationship between happiness and Log(income) is, in reality, not asymptotic.
sentences.zh: 虽然这条曲线看起来是渐近的，并且很快就会达到幸福的最大值，超过这个值，额外的收入就没有什么区别，但幸福与 Log（收入）之间的数学线性关系实际上并不是渐近的。

sentences.en: It will never reach a plateau.
sentences.zh: 它永远不会达到一个平台期。

sentences.en: There may well be some income threshold in the real world beyond which happiness fails to increase, but the point to recognize here is that the existence of a plateau is not predicted mathematically by a logarithmic association.
sentences.zh: 现实世界中很可能存在某个收入门槛，超过该门槛幸福感就无法增加，但这里要认识到的一点是，稳定期的存在并不是通过对数关联来数学预测的。

blocks.html_en: For example, one might look at Fig. 2 and conclude that beyond $400,000/y more money makes very little difference, but if we extrapolated the same fit line to a much higher income range and plotted happiness for incomes ranging from $500,000/y to $15 million/y, one might then be tempted to conclude with equal confidence that more money matters, but that beyond $10 million/y more money makes very little difference2.

sentences.en: For example, one might look at Fig. 2 and conclude that beyond $400,000/y more money makes very little difference, but if we extrapolated the same fit line to a much higher income range and plotted happiness for incomes ranging from $500,000/y to $15 million/y, one might then be tempted to conclude with equal confidence that more money matters, but that beyond $10 million/y more money makes very little difference2.
sentences.zh: 例如，人们可能会看图2并得出结论，超过40万美元/年，更多的钱几乎没有什么区别，但如果我们将同样的拟合线外推到更高的收入范围，并绘制从50万美元/年到1500万美元/年的收入范围内的幸福感，那么人们可能会同样有信心地得出这样的结论：更多的钱很重要，但超过1000万美元/年，更多的钱几乎没有什么区别2。

blocks.html_en: Additionally, proportional differences in income would be predicted to have a constant and non-diminishing association with happiness. For example, a 10% difference in income would be associated with exactly the same difference in happiness, regardless of income level. See the Appendix for an extended explanation and a brief review of the math of logarithms applied to income and happiness.

sentences.en: Additionally, proportional differences in income would be predicted to have a constant and non-diminishing association with happiness.
sentences.zh: 此外，预计收入的比例差异与幸福感之间存在持续且非递减的关联。

sentences.en: For example, a 10% difference in income would be associated with exactly the same difference in happiness, regardless of income level.
sentences.zh: 例如，无论收入水平如何，10% 的收入差异都会带来完全相同的幸福感差异。

sentences.en: See the Appendix for an extended explanation and a brief review of the math of logarithms applied to income and happiness.
sentences.zh: 请参阅附录，了解适用于收入和幸福感的对数数学的详细解释和简要回顾。

blocks.html_en: Anytime one plots an exponential relationship on a linear axis there can appear to mathematically-naive eyes to be an impending plateau. But the point at which a plateau appears to begin is arbitrary. We simply cannot trust our intuitive sense that a plateau is impending when underlying variation is exponential.

sentences.en: Anytime one plots an exponential relationship on a linear axis there can appear to mathematically-naive eyes to be an impending plateau.
sentences.zh: 任何时候，只要在线性轴上绘制指数关系，对于数学天真的眼睛来说，就会出现一个即将到来的平台期。

sentences.en: But the point at which a plateau appears to begin is arbitrary.
sentences.zh: 但平稳期开始的时间点是任意的。

sentences.en: We simply cannot trust our intuitive sense that a plateau is impending when underlying variation is exponential.
sentences.zh: 当潜在的变化呈指数级变化时，我们根本无法相信我们的直觉，即平台期即将到来。

blocks.html_en: Is the world flat or curvy?

sentences.en: Is the world flat or curvy?
sentences.zh: 世界是平坦的还是弯曲的？

blocks.html_en: How should one make sense of these two patterns? If happiness varies linearly with Log(income) and we want to extrapolate from that association to make predictions for the real-world (whether purely descriptive predictions or, in some cases, causal predictions), how should we conceptualize the association between happiness and income? As a flat line as in Fig. 1, or an exponentially diminishing curve as in Fig. 2? Is income, and money more generally, something whose implications for happiness would be predicted to diminish exponentially, or stay constant, in real-world decisions? The answer appears to be: Both, but in different contexts. Let us examine them in turn.

sentences.en: How should one make sense of these two patterns?
sentences.zh: 人们应该如何理解这两种模式？

sentences.en: If happiness varies linearly with Log(income) and we want to extrapolate from that association to make predictions for the real-world (whether purely descriptive predictions or, in some cases, causal predictions), how should we conceptualize the association between happiness and income?
sentences.zh: 如果幸福感随 Log（收入）线性变化，并且我们想从该关联中推断出对现实世界的预测（无论是纯粹的描述性预测，还是在某些情况下，因果预测），我们应该如何概念化幸福感和收入之间的关联？

sentences.en: As a flat line as in Fig. 1, or an exponentially diminishing curve as in Fig. 2?
sentences.zh: 是如图 1 所示的平坦直线，还是如图 2 所示的指数递减曲线？

sentences.en: Is income, and money more generally, something whose implications for happiness would be predicted to diminish exponentially, or stay constant, in real-world decisions?
sentences.zh: 在现实世界的决策中，收入（以及更普遍的金钱）对幸福的影响是否会呈指数级减少或保持不变？

sentences.en: The answer appears to be: Both, but in different contexts.
sentences.zh: 答案似乎是：两者都是，但在不同的背景下。

sentences.en: Let us examine them in turn.
sentences.zh: 让我们依次考察它们。

blocks.html_en: Point 3: Individual incomes differed exponentially in size, offsetting the declining marginal value of dollars. As a result, larger real-world incomes were associated with non-diminishing increments in both Log(income) and happiness.

sentences.en: Point 3: Individual incomes differed exponentially in size, offsetting the declining marginal value of dollars.
sentences.zh: 第三点：个人收入规模呈指数级差异，抵消了美元边际价值的下降。

sentences.en: As a result, larger real-world incomes were associated with non-diminishing increments in both Log(income) and happiness.
sentences.zh: 因此，现实世界中较高的收入与 Log（收入）和幸福感的非递减增量相关。

blocks.html_en: Perhaps the most obvious reason to be interested in the shape of the association between income and happiness is to know whether larger incomes have diminishing returns for happiness, or not.

sentences.en: Perhaps the most obvious reason to be interested in the shape of the association between income and happiness is to know whether larger incomes have diminishing returns for happiness, or not.
sentences.zh: 也许对收入与幸福之间的关系感兴趣的最明显原因是想知道较高的收入是否会导致幸福的回报递减。

blocks.html_en: If dollars get exponentially less valuable when you have more of them, one might assume that the benefits of a larger income also diminish rapidly, even if no true plateau is reached. Indeed, this is a common assumption when people notice happiness plotted against a logarithmic income axis.

sentences.en: If dollars get exponentially less valuable when you have more of them, one might assume that the benefits of a larger income also diminish rapidly, even if no true plateau is reached.
sentences.zh: 如果当你拥有更多美元时，美元的价值就会呈指数级下降，人们可能会认为，即使没有达到真正的稳定状态，收入增加的好处也会迅速减少。

sentences.en: Indeed, this is a common assumption when people notice happiness plotted against a logarithmic income axis.
sentences.zh: 事实上，当人们注意到幸福感与对数收入轴相对应时，这是一个常见的假设。

blocks.html_en: But this assumption depends on an entirely separate feature of reality that is sometimes not considered: How the sizes of incomes vary in the real-world.

sentences.en: But this assumption depends on an entirely separate feature of reality that is sometimes not considered: How the sizes of incomes vary in the real-world.
sentences.zh: 但这种假设取决于现实中一个完全独立的特征，而这个特征有时没有被考虑到：现实世界中收入规模如何变化。

blocks.html_en: So what does the distribution of real-world incomes look like? One possibility is that values of Log(income) differ mainly at the lower end of the income distribution, with comparatively little differentiation amongst high earners. If this were true, we might reasonably expect that most of the room for happiness to rise with income is at the lower end of the income distribution. A second possibility is that incomes themselves differ exponentially in size and that values of Log(income) differ just as much at the high end of the income distribution as at the low end of the income distribution. If that were true, we would not have reason to expect a decline in the marginal utility of a larger real-world income.

sentences.en: So what does the distribution of real-world incomes look like?
sentences.zh: 那么现实世界的收入分配是什么样的呢？

sentences.en: One possibility is that values of Log(income) differ mainly at the lower end of the income distribution, with comparatively little differentiation amongst high earners.
sentences.zh: 一种可能性是，Log（收入）的值主要在收入分布的低端有所不同，而高收入者之间的差异相对较小。

sentences.en: If this were true, we might reasonably expect that most of the room for happiness to rise with income is at the lower end of the income distribution.
sentences.zh: 如果这是真的，我们可以合理地预期，幸福感随着收入而上升的大部分空间都位于收入分配的低端。

sentences.en: A second possibility is that incomes themselves differ exponentially in size and that values of Log(income) differ just as much at the high end of the income distribution as at the low end of the income distribution.
sentences.zh: 第二种可能性是，收入本身的大小呈指数级差异，并且 Log(收入) 值在收入分布的高端和低端的差异同样大。

sentences.en: If that were true, we would not have reason to expect a decline in the marginal utility of a larger real-world income.
sentences.zh: 如果这是真的，我们就没有理由预期现实世界收入增加的边际效用会下降。

blocks.html_en: Evidence strongly favored the latter possibility. If we rank people in order of income, we can view income levels based on their position in the income distribution. A plot of income across 20 income quantiles reveals that the value of Log(income) increases just as steeply across high incomes as low incomes (r = 0.98, see Fig. S1). Data on incomes from the U.S. Congressional Budget Office show a virtually identical income distribution (see Fig. S2), showing that this pattern reflects a realistic income distribution, not something idiosyncratic to this sample of people.

sentences.en: Evidence strongly favored the latter possibility.
sentences.zh: 证据强烈支持后一种可能性。

sentences.en: If we rank people in order of income, we can view income levels based on their position in the income distribution.
sentences.zh: 如果我们按照收入顺序对人们进行排名，我们就可以根据他们在收入分配中的位置来查看收入水平。

sentences.en: A plot of income across 20 income quantiles reveals that the value of Log(income) increases just as steeply across high incomes as low incomes (r = 0.98, see Fig. S1).
sentences.zh: 20 个收入分位数的收入图显示，Log(收入) 值在高收入和低收入中均急剧增加（r = 0.98，见图 S1）。

sentences.en: Data on incomes from the U.S. Congressional Budget Office show a virtually identical income distribution (see Fig. S2), showing that this pattern reflects a realistic income distribution, not something idiosyncratic to this sample of people.
sentences.zh: 美国国会预算办公室的收入数据显示了几乎相同的收入分配（见图 S2），表明这种模式反映了现实的收入分配，而不是该人群样本中的特殊情况。

blocks.html_en: Now the real question: Does the association between happiness and one’s position in the distribution of real-world incomes (income quantile) look more like Fig. 1, or more like Fig. 2? Is it consistent with diminishing marginal value of a larger income, or not?

sentences.en: Now the real question: Does the association between happiness and one’s position in the distribution of real-world incomes (income quantile) look more like Fig. 1, or more like Fig. 2?
sentences.zh: 现在真正的问题是：幸福与一个人在现实世界收入分配中的地位（收入分位数）之间的关联看起来更像图 1，还是更像图 2？

sentences.en: Is it consistent with diminishing marginal value of a larger income, or not?
sentences.zh: 它是否与较高收入的边际价值递减相一致？

blocks.html_en: The results are clear. Income quantile had a linear association with happiness (see Fig. 3). As a result, it’s possible to reproduce the linear relationship observed between income and happiness without a logarithmic transformation. All one needs to do is plot a person’s position in the real-world income distribution. The group-level correlation between income quantile (range: 1 to 20) and mean experienced happiness for each quantile is r = 0.96, with a highly linear shape. The results are comparably linear for the three life satisfaction measures (r = 0.98-0.99). This pattern is consistent with the possibility that moving up to the next highest income in the real-world would have the same relationship to happiness, regardless of one’s position in the distribution of incomes. For example, it suggests that a higher-paying occupation or job would be associated with similar gains to happiness regardless of income level (at least within the range of incomes observed here)3.

sentences.en: The results are clear.
sentences.zh: 结果很明显。

sentences.en: Income quantile had a linear association with happiness (see Fig. 3).
sentences.zh: 收入分位数与幸福感呈线性相关（见图 3）。

sentences.en: As a result, it’s possible to reproduce the linear relationship observed between income and happiness without a logarithmic transformation.
sentences.zh: 因此，无需对数变换就可以重现收入和幸福之间观察到的线性关系。

sentences.en: All one needs to do is plot a person’s position in the real-world income distribution.
sentences.zh: 人们所需要做的就是绘制一个人在现实世界收入分配中的位置。

sentences.en: The group-level correlation between income quantile (range: 1 to 20) and mean experienced happiness for each quantile is r = 0.96, with a highly linear shape.
sentences.zh: 收入分位数（范围：1 至 20）与每个分位数的平均幸福感之间的群体层面相关性为 r = 0.96，具有高度线性形状。

sentences.en: The results are comparably linear for the three life satisfaction measures (r = 0.98-0.99).
sentences.zh: 三种生活满意度指标的结果具有相当的线性关系 (r = 0.98-0.99)。

sentences.en: This pattern is consistent with the possibility that moving up to the next highest income in the real-world would have the same relationship to happiness, regardless of one’s position in the distribution of incomes.
sentences.zh: 这种模式与这样一种可能性是一致的：无论一个人在收入分配中的地位如何，在现实世界中达到下一个最高收入与幸福感具有相同的关系。

sentences.en: For example, it suggests that a higher-paying occupation or job would be associated with similar gains to happiness regardless of income level (at least within the range of incomes observed here)3.
sentences.zh: 例如，它表明，无论收入水平如何（至少在此处观察到的收入范围内），高薪职业或工作都会与相似的幸福感相关3。

blocks.html_en: Fig. 3. Mean levels of happiness, including experienced happiness (real-time feelings) and overall life satisfaction, across 20 income quantiles. The group-level correlations between happiness and income quantile is displayed.

sentences.en: Fig. 3.
Mean levels of happiness, including experienced happiness (real-time feelings) and overall life satisfaction, across 20 income quantiles.
sentences.zh: 20 个收入分位数的平均幸福水平，包括体验到的幸福感（实时感受）和整体生活满意度。

sentences.en: The group-level correlations between happiness and income quantile is displayed.
sentences.zh: 显示幸福感与收入分位数之间的群体级别相关性。

blocks.html_en: These results collectively show that income itself varies exponentially in size across the income distribution, to such a degree that it counteracts the exponentially diminishing marginal value of money when it comes to predicting happiness. Each step up the ladder of real-world incomes was associated with a non-diminishing increment in Log(income) and a non-diminishing increment in happiness.

sentences.en: These results collectively show that income itself varies exponentially in size across the income distribution, to such a degree that it counteracts the exponentially diminishing marginal value of money when it comes to predicting happiness.
sentences.zh: 这些结果共同表明，收入本身在收入分配中的规模呈指数级变化，其程度足以抵消在预测幸福感时呈指数级递减的货币边际价值。

sentences.en: Each step up the ladder of real-world incomes was associated with a non-diminishing increment in Log(income) and a non-diminishing increment in happiness.
sentences.zh: 现实世界收入阶梯的每一步都与 Log（收入）的非递减增量和幸福感的非递减增量相关。

blocks.html_en: The concavity one sees when plotting income on a linear axis (as in Fig. 2) can therefore be doubly misleading: not only does it appear to suggest the possibility of an asymptote or plateau in happiness when none exists mathematically, but it also fails to capture the exponentially growing sizes of people’s incomes.

sentences.en: The concavity one sees when plotting income on a linear axis (as in Fig. 2) can therefore be doubly misleading: not only does it appear to suggest the possibility of an asymptote or plateau in happiness when none exists mathematically, but it also fails to capture the exponentially growing sizes of people’s incomes.
sentences.zh: 因此，人们在线性轴上绘制收入时所看到的凹性（如图 2 所示）可能会产生双重误导：它不仅似乎表明幸福指数可能存在渐近线或稳定期（而数学上并不存在），而且它也无法反映人们收入呈指数级增长的规模。

blocks.html_en: Point 4: Exponential differences in happiness are predicted in other domains, including large financial risks and trade-offs between people with unequal incomes.

sentences.en: Point 4: Exponential differences in happiness are predicted in other domains, including large financial risks and trade-offs between people with unequal incomes.
sentences.zh: 第四点：在其他领域，包括巨大的金融风险和收入不平等的人们之间的权衡，幸福感也会出现指数级差异。

blocks.html_en: The preceding analysis shows that, despite the exponential decline in the marginal value of a dollar for happiness, individuals nevertheless exhibit a linear, non-diminishing association between larger real-world incomes and greater happiness. The same would be predicted for proportional variation in income, such as a 5% change in income for an individual, or a 5% change applied equiproportionately to a population (10). Yet that pattern does not apply to every context. There are other contexts where the declining marginal utility of money would indeed be expected to have exponential implications.

sentences.en: The preceding analysis shows that, despite the exponential decline in the marginal value of a dollar for happiness, individuals nevertheless exhibit a linear, non-diminishing association between larger real-world incomes and greater happiness.
sentences.zh: 前面的分析表明，尽管一美元带来的幸福感的边际价值呈指数下降，但个人在现实世界中较高的收入和较高的幸福感之间仍然表现出线性的、非递减的关联。

sentences.en: The same would be predicted for proportional variation in income, such as a 5% change in income for an individual, or a 5% change applied equiproportionately to a population (10).
sentences.zh: 对于收入的比例变化也有同样的预测，例如个人收入变化 5%，或人口平均变化 5% (10)。

sentences.en: Yet that pattern does not apply to every context.
sentences.zh: 然而这种模式并不适用于所有情况。

sentences.en: There are other contexts where the declining marginal utility of money would indeed be expected to have exponential implications.
sentences.zh: 在其他情况下，货币边际效用的下降确实会产生指数级的影响。

blocks.html_en: Let us assume for the sake of following hypothetical examples that (i) the association between happiness and Log(income) is linear across the entire range of possible incomes (including incomes higher and lower than those observed here), (ii) the association reflects a causal relationship where more money produces greater happiness, (iii) we are only modeling the effects of income on happiness (ignoring all other considerations for simplicity), and (iv) units of reported happiness represent the same amount of happiness, on average, at different income levels. Although we will assume the sign (positive) and shape (linear) of the relationship, we do not need to assume anything about its magnitude: Everything that follows is independent of the magnitude of the alleged effect of money on happiness. Some of these assumptions are likely to be violated to some extent in the real-world, of course, but contemplating a version of the world where they are precisely true makes it possible to explore the implications of a linear association between happiness and Log(income).

sentences.en: Let us assume for the sake of following hypothetical examples that (i) the association between happiness and Log(income) is linear across the entire range of possible incomes (including incomes higher and lower than those observed here), (ii) the association reflects a causal relationship where more money produces greater happiness, (iii) we are only modeling the effects of income on happiness (ignoring all other considerations for simplicity), and (iv) units of reported happiness represent the same amount of happiness, on average, at different income levels.
sentences.zh: 为了下面的假设例子，让我们假设（i）幸福与Log（收入）之间的关联在整个可能收入范围（包括高于和低于此处观察到的收入）之间是线性的，（ii）这种关联反映了一种因果关系，即更多的钱产生更大的幸福，（iii）我们仅对收入对幸福的影响进行建模（为了简单起见，忽略所有其他考虑因素），以及（iv）报告的幸福单位平均代表不同收入水平的相同数量的幸福。

sentences.en: Although we will assume the sign (positive) and shape (linear) of the relationship, we do not need to assume anything about its magnitude: Everything that follows is independent of the magnitude of the alleged effect of money on happiness.
sentences.zh: 尽管我们将假设该关系的符号（正）和形状（线性），但我们不需要对其大小做出任何假设：接下来的一切都与所谓的金钱对幸福的影响的大小无关。

sentences.en: Some of these assumptions are likely to be violated to some extent in the real-world, of course, but contemplating a version of the world where they are precisely true makes it possible to explore the implications of a linear association between happiness and Log(income).
sentences.zh: 当然，其中一些假设在现实世界中可能会在某种程度上被违反，但考虑到这些假设完全正确的世界版本，使得探索幸福与 Log（收入）之间线性关联的含义成为可能。

blocks.html_en: One individual context where the exponential decline in the marginal value of dollars for happiness could be important are high-stakes risky bets. This takes us back to the type of scenario Bernoulli contemplated in 1738 when he speculated that utility scaled with the logarithm of wealth (1), even though at the time he was focused on explaining people’s risk-averse behavior (at which his theory ultimately fares poorly (21)), rather than predicting their happiness (at which his theory appears to fare remarkably well).

sentences.en: One individual context where the exponential decline in the marginal value of dollars for happiness could be important are high-stakes risky bets.
sentences.zh: 美元对幸福的边际价值呈指数下降可能很重要的一种具体情况是高风险的赌注。

sentences.en: This takes us back to the type of scenario Bernoulli contemplated in 1738 when he speculated that utility scaled with the logarithm of wealth (1), even though at the time he was focused on explaining people’s risk-averse behavior (at which his theory ultimately fares poorly (21)), rather than predicting their happiness (at which his theory appears to fare remarkably well).
sentences.zh: 这让我们回到了伯努利在 1738 年设想的场景类型，当时他推测效用与财富的对数成正比 (1)，尽管当时他专注于解释人们的风险规避行为（在这一点上他的理论最终表现不佳 (21)），而不是预测他们的幸福感（在这方面他的理论似乎表现得非常好）。

blocks.html_en: Consider a hypothetical person who has permanently retired with a pension of $50,000/y and has no other income or savings. If this person is given the choice between two options: a “safe option” with a 100% chance that their income doubles to $100,000/y, and a “risky option” that has a 90% chance of failure (their income remains $50,000/y) and a 10% chance of success and a larger payoff. How big does the risky payoff need to be to equate its expected value for happiness?

sentences.en: Consider a hypothetical person who has permanently retired with a pension of $50,000/y and has no other income or savings.
sentences.zh: 考虑一个假设的人，他已经永久退休，养老金为每年 50,000 美元，没有其他收入或储蓄。

sentences.en: If this person is given the choice between two options: a “safe option” with a 100% chance that their income doubles to $100,000/y, and a “risky option” that has a 90% chance of failure (their income remains $50,000/y) and a 10% chance of success and a larger payoff.
sentences.zh: 如果让这个人在两个选项之间进行选择：“安全选项”，其收入有 100% 的机会翻倍至 100,000 美元/年；“风险选项”，有 90% 的失败机会（他们的收入仍为 50,000 美元/年），但有 10% 的成功机会和更大的回报。

sentences.en: How big does the risky payoff need to be to equate its expected value for happiness?
sentences.zh: 风险回报需要有多大才能与幸福的预期价值相等？

blocks.html_en: One’s first instinct might be to assume the risky payoff needs to be ten times as large at $500,000/y (plus the original $50,000/y). But that is not nearly enough. This equates the expected value in dollars ($100,000/y), but it doesn’t come anywhere close to equating the expected value for happiness. To achieve parity with a guaranteed income of $100,000/y, the risky payoff with a 10% chance of success would have to be approximately $50,000,000/y!

sentences.en: One’s first instinct might be to assume the risky payoff needs to be ten times as large at $500,000/y (plus the original $50,000/y).
sentences.zh: 人们的第一直觉可能是假设风险回报需要是每年 500,000 美元（加上最初的 50,000 美元/年）的十倍。

sentences.en: But that is not nearly enough.
sentences.zh: 但这还远远不够。

sentences.en: This equates the expected value in dollars ($100,000/y), but it doesn’t come anywhere close to equating the expected value for happiness.
sentences.zh: 这相当于美元的预期价值（100,000 美元/年），但它与幸福的预期价值相差甚远。

sentences.en: To achieve parity with a guaranteed income of $100,000/y, the risky payoff with a 10% chance of success would have to be approximately $50,000,000/y!
sentences.zh: 为了实现每年 100,000 美元的保证收入，成功机会为 10% 的风险回报必须约为每年 50,000,000 美元！

blocks.html_en: Why is this? Each additional dollar is exponentially less valuable, so a lot more money is needed. To match the predicted happiness associated with the safe option (the guaranteed doubling of income) with a 10% chance of a bigger payoff, the risky payoff would need to be 210 = 1,024 times current income, or over $50,000,000/y. The reason is that equating the expected utility of the safe and risky payoffs requires the following:

sentences.en: Why is this?
sentences.zh: 这是为什么呢？

sentences.en: Each additional dollar is exponentially less valuable, so a lot more money is needed.
sentences.zh: 每增加一美元的价值就会呈指数级下降，因此需要更多的钱。

sentences.en: To match the predicted happiness associated with the safe option (the guaranteed doubling of income) with a 10% chance of a bigger payoff, the risky payoff would need to be 210 = 1,024 times current income, or over $50,000,000/y.
sentences.zh: 为了将与安全选项相关的预测幸福感（保证收入翻倍）与 10% 的更大回报机会相匹配，风险回报需要为当前收入的 210 = 1,024 倍，或每年超过 50,000,000 美元。

sentences.en: The reason is that equating the expected utility of the safe and risky payoffs requires the following:
sentences.zh: 原因是，将安全收益和风险收益的预期效用等同起来需要满足以下条件：

blocks.html_en: 100% * Log(2) = 10% * Log(risky payoff)

sentences.en: 100% * Log(2) = 10% * Log(risky payoff)
sentences.zh: 100% * Log(2) = 10% * Log（风险收益）

blocks.html_en: …which is only true if the risky payoff is 210 times current income. If the risky option had a 50% chance of success, the risky payoff would need to be a more modest 22 times current income, or $200,000/y (a $150,000/y increase). If the risky option had only a 1% chance of success, the risky payoff would need to be 2100 times current income, or far more than all money in circulation, to achieve parity for happiness. Exponential indeed. Working for many years at a risky start-up at a low salary or spending money on lottery tickets may be even less rational than they appear, if we are willing to reduce the calculation to the pattern described here.

sentences.en: …which is only true if the risky payoff is 210 times current income.
sentences.zh: ……只有当风险收益是当前收入的 210 倍时，这才是正确的。

sentences.en: If the risky option had a 50% chance of success, the risky payoff would need to be a more modest 22 times current income, or $200,000/y (a $150,000/y increase).
sentences.zh: 如果有风险的选择有 50% 的成功机会，则风险回报需要是当前收入的 22 倍，即每年 200,000 美元（每年增加 150,000 美元）。

sentences.en: If the risky option had only a 1% chance of success, the risky payoff would need to be 2100 times current income, or far more than all money in circulation, to achieve parity for happiness.
sentences.zh: 如果有风险的选择只有 1% 的成功机会，那么风险回报将需要是当前收入的 2100 倍，或者远远超过流通中的所有货币，才能实现幸福平等。

sentences.en: Exponential indeed.
sentences.zh: 确实是指数级的。

sentences.en: Working for many years at a risky start-up at a low salary or spending money on lottery tickets may be even less rational than they appear, if we are willing to reduce the calculation to the pattern described here.
sentences.zh: 如果我们愿意将计算简化为此处描述的模式，那么在一家高风险的初创公司以低薪工作多年或花钱买彩票可能比看起来更不理性。

blocks.html_en: If we consider risks in the domain of losses instead of gains, the same simple relationship shows why insurance could be expected to be beneficial for happiness when the downside risk is very large. If a person risks losing a substantial fraction of their total lifetime earnings (such as in the case of a house fire or permanent disability), then even insurance sold at a price profitable to an insurer (i.e., with a negative expected monetary value for the consumer) could be quite beneficial to the consumer’s well-being (i.e., with a positive expected value for happiness). In such cases, insurance premiums are purchased with exponentially less valuable dollars than the dollars against whose loss they protect against.

sentences.en: If we consider risks in the domain of losses instead of gains, the same simple relationship shows why insurance could be expected to be beneficial for happiness when the downside risk is very large.
sentences.zh: 如果我们考虑损失而不是收益领域的风险，同样简单的关系说明了为什么当下行风险非常大时，保险可以预期对幸福有益。

sentences.en: If a person risks losing a substantial fraction of their total lifetime earnings (such as in the case of a house fire or permanent disability), then even insurance sold at a price profitable to an insurer (i.e., with a negative expected monetary value for the consumer) could be quite beneficial to the consumer’s well-being (i.e., with a positive expected value for happiness).
sentences.zh: 如果一个人面临着损失其一生总收入的很大一部分的风险（例如在房屋火灾或永久性残疾的情况下），那么即使以保险公司有利可图的价格出售保险（即消费者的预期货币价值为负）也可能对消费者的福祉非常有益（即幸福的预期价值为正）。

sentences.en: In such cases, insurance premiums are purchased with exponentially less valuable dollars than the dollars against whose loss they protect against.
sentences.zh: 在这种情况下，购买保险费的美元价值远低于其所保护的损失的美元价值。

blocks.html_en: The fact that insurance can be justified by declining marginal utility is not at all a new point, of course - it is a fundamental rationale of insurance against major risks. In fact, it is a mathematical consequence of a logarithmic utility curve that Bernoulli himself explored in his 1738 paper. However, the present results suggest it could potentially be justified and calculated from real-world happiness data, as opposed to theoretical assumptions about an opaque conception of utility.

sentences.en: The fact that insurance can be justified by declining marginal utility is not at all a new point, of course - it is a fundamental rationale of insurance against major risks.
sentences.zh: 当然，保险可以通过边际效用下降来证明其合理性这一事实并不是什么新鲜事，它是针对重大风险的保险的基本原理。

sentences.en: In fact, it is a mathematical consequence of a logarithmic utility curve that Bernoulli himself explored in his 1738 paper.
sentences.zh: 事实上，这是伯努利本人在 1738 年论文中探索的对数效用曲线的数学结果。

sentences.en: However, the present results suggest it could potentially be justified and calculated from real-world happiness data, as opposed to theoretical assumptions about an opaque conception of utility.
sentences.zh: 然而，目前的结果表明，它可能是合理的，并根据现实世界的幸福数据进行计算，而不是关于不透明的效用概念的理论假设。

blocks.html_en: However, there’s another important context to consider. Exponential differences are especially apparent, and perhaps most consequential, in situations that involve trade-offs between people with very different incomes. If dollars become exponentially less valuable as incomes rise, then the well-being benefits of a given dollar are predicted to be exponentially larger for a poorer person than a richer person.

sentences.en: However, there’s another important context to consider.
sentences.zh: 然而，还有另一个重要的背景需要考虑。

sentences.en: Exponential differences are especially apparent, and perhaps most consequential, in situations that involve trade-offs between people with very different incomes.
sentences.zh: 在涉及收入差异很大的人之间进行权衡的情况下，指数差异尤其明显，而且可能是最重要的。

sentences.en: If dollars become exponentially less valuable as incomes rise, then the well-being benefits of a given dollar are predicted to be exponentially larger for a poorer person than a richer person.
sentences.zh: 如果随着收入的增加，美元的价值呈指数级下降，那么对于穷人来说，特定美元的福利收益预计会比富人指数级增加。

blocks.html_en: Take philanthropy. Let us assume that money can be efficiently invested to improve the lives of people who are poor on the global scale (e.g., earning $2/day), and that there is a 1:1 monetary cost:benefit ratio. Perhaps money could be used to invest in health care, nutrition, education, or job training, or simply delivered directly via unconditional cash transfer. This hypothetical example simply assumes $1 of benefit to the beneficiary’s income for each $1 invested without regard to mechanism. Under these assumptions, a dollar contributed by a person earning $73,000/y that increases the income of a person earning $2/day ($730/y) would generate a predicted ROI of approximately 10,000% for happiness4. Why? Because their earnings differ by a factor of 100, the benefit to happiness of that dollar is estimated to be 100 times greater for the person earning $2/day than the loss incurred by the person earning $73,000/y (for an experimental test of a similar idea, see (14)).

sentences.en: Take philanthropy.
sentences.zh: 以慈善事业为例。

sentences.en: Under these assumptions, a dollar contributed by a person earning $73,000/y that increases the income of a person earning $2/day ($730/y) would generate a predicted ROI of approximately 10,000% for happiness4.
sentences.zh: 在这些假设下，年收入 73,000 美元的人贡献一美元，增加每天收入 2 美元（730 美元/年）的人的收入，将产生大约 10,000% 的幸福投资回报率 4。

sentences.en: Because their earnings differ by a factor of 100, the benefit to happiness of that dollar is estimated to be 100 times greater for the person earning $2/day than the loss incurred by the person earning $73,000/y (for an experimental test of a similar idea, see (14)).
sentences.zh: 因为他们的收入相差 100 倍，所以对于每天收入 2 美元的人来说，这一美元带来的幸福感估计是年收入 73,000 美元的人所遭受的损失的 100 倍（对于类似想法的实验测试，请参见 (14)）。

blocks.html_en: What if we take the example to the extreme and consider a hypothetical superwealthy person who earns $1 billion/y and decides to voluntarily contribute to people earning $2/day, under otherwise identical assumptions (since we are only modeling the effects of income, for simplicity let us assume this person has a very large income stream and ignore wealth). Now, the predicted ROI of the first dollar is nearly 137,000,000%! Or to play out a more complete example, the billionaire contributing 10% of her income to double the incomes of people earning $2/day would be expected to generate ≈ 90,000,000% return on investment for collective human happiness5. No investor could ordinarily achieve a 90,000,000% rate of return on such a large amount of money. If they did, it would mean turning that $100 million into over $90 trillion in one year’s time, an amount approximately equal to the entire world’s GDP in 2021.

sentences.zh: 如果我们把这个例子推向极端，考虑一个假设的超级富豪，他每年收入 10 亿美元，并决定自愿为每天收入 2 美元的人捐款，在其他方面相同的假设下（因为我们只是对收入的影响进行建模，为简单起见，让我们假设这个人有非常大的收入流并忽略财富）会怎么样？

blocks.html_en: The same logic could extend to many other situations. For example, decision-makers that seek to improve collective happiness might find that preferentially increasing incomes of those who earn the least generates a large ROI for collective happiness. In the domain of compensation, an employer allocating marginal dollars to raise the incomes of employees earning $30,000/y is predicted to generate 20x (2,000%) as much marginal happiness as raising the incomes of employees earning $600,000/y, and 200x (20,000%) as much marginal happiness as raising the incomes of employees earning $6,000,000/y. Organizations sometimes do just the opposite, paying large bonuses to highly-paid executives rather than directing discretionary compensation to the people on whom it would be expected to have the largest impact. Similarly, nations sometimes apply relatively low tax rates on their richest citizens, even though money appears to be exponentially more important to the well-being of those who earn less.

sentences.en: The same logic could extend to many other situations.
sentences.zh: 同样的逻辑可以扩展到许多其他情况。

sentences.en: For example, decision-makers that seek to improve collective happiness might find that preferentially increasing incomes of those who earn the least generates a large ROI for collective happiness.
sentences.zh: 例如，寻求提高集体幸福感的决策者可能会发现，优先增加收入最少的人的收入可以为集体幸福带来巨大的投资回报率。

sentences.en: In the domain of compensation, an employer allocating marginal dollars to raise the incomes of employees earning $30,000/y is predicted to generate 20x (2,000%) as much marginal happiness as raising the incomes of employees earning $600,000/y, and 200x (20,000%) as much marginal happiness as raising the incomes of employees earning $6,000,000/y.
sentences.zh: 在薪酬领域，预计雇主分配边际美元来提高年收入 30,000 美元员工的收入所产生的边际幸福感是提高年收入 600,000 美元员工收入的 20 倍（2,000%），是提高年收入 6,000,000 美元员工收入的 200 倍（20,000%）边际幸福感。

sentences.en: Organizations sometimes do just the opposite, paying large bonuses to highly-paid executives rather than directing discretionary compensation to the people on whom it would be expected to have the largest impact.
sentences.zh: 组织有时会采取相反的做法，向高薪高管支付巨额奖金，而不是将可自由支配的薪酬发放给预计对其影响最大的人员。

sentences.en: Similarly, nations sometimes apply relatively low tax rates on their richest citizens, even though money appears to be exponentially more important to the well-being of those who earn less.
sentences.zh: 同样，国家有时对其最富有的公民实行相对较低的税率，尽管金钱对于收入较少的人的福祉似乎更为重要。

blocks.html_en: The above numbers and predictions are based on simplistic assumptions that are sure to be violated to at least some extent in the real world, of course. In practice, ROI values could be larger or smaller than calculated here, or even backfire and become negative in some cases. And in dynamic systems like an economy or a society, long-term consequences can be hard to predict even when short-term effects are known. Needless to say, accurate predictions of the consequences of any specific action lies far beyond the scope of this paper. The point is not the precise values but, instead, the simple observation that there are large and calculable implications of a linear relationship between happiness and Log(income) when trade-offs are made between people with unequal incomes, and that these mathematical implications can play out quite differently than they do for an individual person navigating different income levels.

sentences.en: The above numbers and predictions are based on simplistic assumptions that are sure to be violated to at least some extent in the real world, of course.
sentences.zh: 当然，上述数字和预测基于简单化的假设，这些假设在现实世界中至少在某种程度上肯定会被违反。

sentences.en: In practice, ROI values could be larger or smaller than calculated here, or even backfire and become negative in some cases.
sentences.zh: 实际上，ROI 值可能比此处计算的更大或更小，甚至在某些情况下会适得其反并变为负值。

sentences.zh: 重点不在于精确的数值，而在于简单的观察，即当收入不平等的人之间进行权衡时，幸福与 Log（收入）之间的线性关系存在巨大且可计算的含义，并且这些数学含义可能与个人在不同收入水平下的表现截然不同。

blocks.html_en: In the most prominent cases involving money and happiness, the world is flat (like Fig. 1) for people making individual decisions, but the world is curvy (like Fig. 2) for people making collective decisions.

sentences.en: In the most prominent cases involving money and happiness, the world is flat (like Fig. 1) for people making individual decisions, but the world is curvy (like Fig. 2) for people making collective decisions.
sentences.zh: 在涉及金钱和幸福的最突出案例中，对于做出个人决策的人们来说，世界是平坦的（如图 1 所示），但对于做出集体决策的人们来说，世界是弯曲的（如图 2 所示）。

blocks.html_en: Point 5: Money’s diverging implications for happiness, linear in some contexts but exponential in others, could help explain why income inequality persists as societies get richer, why the income distribution is shaped the way it is, and why happiness in the U.S. has not seen more improvement in recent decades.

sentences.en: Point 5: Money’s diverging implications for happiness, linear in some contexts but exponential in others, could help explain why income inequality persists as societies get richer, why the income distribution is shaped the way it is, and why happiness in the U.S. has not seen more improvement in recent decades.
sentences.zh: 第5点：金钱对幸福感的不同影响，在某些情况下是线性的，但在另一些情况下是指数级的，这有助于解释为什么收入不平等随着社会变得更加富裕而持续存在，为什么收入分配是这样形成的，以及为什么美国的幸福感在近几十年来没有看到更多改善。

blocks.html_en: Consider a world quite different from the one we appear to inhabit. If, contrary of the pattern described here, the payoff of a higher income for individual happiness (i.e., its marginal utility) did exponentially diminish, then we would have some reason to expect a trend towards greater income equality over time if a society becomes richer. Why? For a well-off individual in that hypothetical world, there would be less and less to gain from a higher income and it would make sense progressively deprioritize a higher income relative to other priorities, at least from the standpoint of happiness. If such a society got richer over time, there would be some reason to expect this to disproportionately raise the bottom of the income distribution as low earners seek to improve their financial lot and, accordingly, their happiness, leading to ever-decreasing income inequality. This suggests the potential for a harmonious relationship between individual and collective happiness in relation to money, since the pursuit of individual and collective happiness would tend to converge as a society got richer. But that does not appear to be the reality we live in, at least within the range of incomes observed here.

sentences.en: Consider a world quite different from the one we appear to inhabit.
sentences.zh: 想象一个与我们看似居住的世界截然不同的世界。

sentences.en: If, contrary of the pattern described here, the payoff of a higher income for individual happiness (i.e., its marginal utility) did exponentially diminish, then we would have some reason to expect a trend towards greater income equality over time if a society becomes richer.

sentences.en: Why?
sentences.zh: 为什么？

sentences.en: For a well-off individual in that hypothetical world, there would be less and less to gain from a higher income and it would make sense progressively deprioritize a higher income relative to other priorities, at least from the standpoint of happiness.
sentences.zh: 对于那个假设世界中的富裕个人来说，从较高收入中获得的收益会越来越少，并且相对于其他优先事项，逐渐降低较高收入的优先级是有意义的，至少从幸福的角度来看是这样。

sentences.en: If such a society got richer over time, there would be some reason to expect this to disproportionately raise the bottom of the income distribution as low earners seek to improve their financial lot and, accordingly, their happiness, leading to ever-decreasing income inequality.
sentences.zh: 如果这样的社会随着时间的推移而变得更加富裕，那么就有理由预计这将不成比例地提高收入分配的底部，因为低收入者寻求改善他们的财务状况，从而提高他们的幸福感，从而导致收入不平等不断缩小。

sentences.en: This suggests the potential for a harmonious relationship between individual and collective happiness in relation to money, since the pursuit of individual and collective happiness would tend to converge as a society got richer.
sentences.zh: 这表明个人和集体幸福与金钱之间存在和谐关系的潜力，因为随着社会变得更加富裕，对个人和集体幸福的追求往往会趋于一致。

sentences.en: But that does not appear to be the reality we live in, at least within the range of incomes observed here.
sentences.zh: 但这似乎并不是我们生活的现实，至少在我们观察到的收入范围内是这样。

blocks.html_en: In actual reality, the expected payoff of a larger real-world income for happiness appears to remain approximately constant, on average, such that it could be entirely reasonable for an individual to continue aspiring to climb one more rung in the income ladder, if all else is held equal. But since each rung entails exponentially more dollars, the successful pursuit of higher incomes for the well-off could perpetuate inequality, as large quantities of money are allocated to a small group of people who succeed in becoming richer. This could be suboptimal for collective happiness if increases to a large income are achieved primarily through value capture (capturing a bigger slice for oneself without growing the size of the pie) rather than primarily through value creation (getting a bigger slice by creating a bigger pie). When it comes to money, this highlights a potential tension in the geometry of individual and collective happiness.

sentences.en: In actual reality, the expected payoff of a larger real-world income for happiness appears to remain approximately constant, on average, such that it could be entirely reasonable for an individual to continue aspiring to climb one more rung in the income ladder, if all else is held equal.
sentences.zh: 事实上，平均而言，现实世界中较高收入对幸福的预期回报似乎保持大致恒定，因此，如果其他条件相同，个人继续渴望在收入阶梯上再攀登一级是完全合理的。

sentences.en: But since each rung entails exponentially more dollars, the successful pursuit of higher incomes for the well-off could perpetuate inequality, as large quantities of money are allocated to a small group of people who succeed in becoming richer.
sentences.zh: 但由于每一级都需要成倍增加的美元，富人成功追求更高收入可能会导致不平等长期存在，因为大量资金被分配给成功致富的一小部分人。

sentences.en: This could be suboptimal for collective happiness if increases to a large income are achieved primarily through value capture (capturing a bigger slice for oneself without growing the size of the pie) rather than primarily through value creation (getting a bigger slice by creating a bigger pie).
sentences.zh: 如果增加大量收入主要是通过价值获取（在不做大蛋糕的情况下为自己获取更大的份额）而不是主要通过价值创造（通过创造更大的蛋糕来获得更大的份额）来实现，那么这对于集体幸福来说可能不是最理想的。

sentences.en: When it comes to money, this highlights a potential tension in the geometry of individual and collective happiness.
sentences.zh: 当谈到金钱时，这凸显了个人和集体幸福之间潜在的紧张关系。

blocks.html_en: This pattern of results might also help explain why incomes differ exponentially in size in the first place. For example, larger incomes incentivize people to take certain jobs over others, to seek promotions, and to be productive. If happiness varies linearly with Log(income), then to achieve a non-diminishing incentive, incomes might need to grow exponentially larger in size. If they didn’t, the power of money as an incentive (at least in terms of happiness) would diminish dramatically. Some people would surely still choose to be CEOs even if they didn’t earn millions of dollars. But to the extent that a market economy uses money to incentivize behavior, the pattern described here offers a potential explanation for why salaries differ so greatly in size: they vary exponentially in order to maintain a non-diminishing association to happiness. After all, even if human decision-making is subject to a host of quirks and irrationalities, it is affected by incentives, too. This tendency might be accentuated if high-paying jobs also have a larger component of variable compensation (recall the “risky payoff” calculations).

sentences.en: This pattern of results might also help explain why incomes differ exponentially in size in the first place.
sentences.zh: 这种结果模式也可能有助于解释为什么收入规模首先呈指数级差异。

sentences.en: For example, larger incomes incentivize people to take certain jobs over others, to seek promotions, and to be productive.

sentences.en: If happiness varies linearly with Log(income), then to achieve a non-diminishing incentive, incomes might need to grow exponentially larger in size.

sentences.zh: 如果他们不这样做，金钱作为激励的力量（至少在幸福方面）就会急剧减弱。

sentences.en: Some people would surely still choose to be CEOs even if they didn’t earn millions of dollars.
sentences.zh: 即使没有赚到数百万美元，有些人肯定仍然会选择担任首席执行官。

sentences.en: But to the extent that a market economy uses money to incentivize behavior, the pattern described here offers a potential explanation for why salaries differ so greatly in size: they vary exponentially in order to maintain a non-diminishing association to happiness.
sentences.zh: 但就市场经济使用金钱来激励行为而言，这里描述的模式为为什么工资大小差异如此之大提供了一个潜在的解释：它们呈指数级变化，以保持与幸福感的非递减关联。

sentences.en: After all, even if human decision-making is subject to a host of quirks and irrationalities, it is affected by incentives, too.

sentences.en: This tendency might be accentuated if high-paying jobs also have a larger component of variable compensation (recall the “risky payoff” calculations).
sentences.zh: 如果高薪工作也有较大比例的可变薪酬（回想一下“风险回报”计算），这种趋势可能会加剧。

blocks.html_en: Finally, this pattern could at least partly explain a puzzle: happiness in the U.S. has stagnated in recent decades despite growing GDP per capita (22, 23). One contributing reason could be that income growth has been disproportionately concentrated at the top of the income distribution. In recent decades, it’s the highest earners in the U.S. who have experienced the fastest income growth, even in percentage terms (they have gained exponentially more, in dollar terms). In contrast, real (i.e., inflation-adjusted) incomes for lower income folks, where additional dollars would be expected to have an exponentially larger impact on happiness, have grown the slowest for decades (24). This is precisely opposite to the trend these results predict would most benefit a society’s collective happiness. Setting aside the countless complexities that the present model does not account for, if income growth is going to be unequally distributed, these results predict that society’s average happiness would benefit the most if economic growth disproportionately benefited the poorest people instead of disproportionately benefiting the richest. Alternatively, equiproportionate gains, with the same percentage increase throughout the income distribution, would evenly distribute the expected happiness benefits of economic growth.

sentences.en: Finally, this pattern could at least partly explain a puzzle: happiness in the U.S. has stagnated in recent decades despite growing GDP per capita (22, 23).
sentences.zh: 最后，这种模式至少可以部分解释一个谜题：尽管人均 GDP 不断增长，但美国的幸福感在近几十年来却停滞不前 (22, 23)。

sentences.zh: 这与这些结果预测的最有利于社会集体幸福的趋势恰恰相反。

sentences.en: Setting aside the countless complexities that the present model does not account for, if income growth is going to be unequally distributed, these results predict that society’s average happiness would benefit the most if economic growth disproportionately benefited the poorest people instead of disproportionately benefiting the richest.
sentences.zh: 抛开当前模型没有考虑到的无数复杂性，如果收入增长分布不均，这些结果预测，如果经济增长不成比例地惠及最贫困人口而不是不成比例地惠及最富有的人，那么社会的平均幸福感将受益最多。

sentences.en: Alternatively, equiproportionate gains, with the same percentage increase throughout the income distribution, would evenly distribute the expected happiness benefits of economic growth.

blocks.html_en: Discussion

sentences.en: Discussion
sentences.zh: 讨论

blocks.html_en: Understanding the association between income and happiness has the potential to help us make decisions that support human well-being. While the association between income and happiness is one of the most studied topics in the happiness literature, it is common even for researchers to disagree about what it means for happiness to vary with Log(income), and sometimes even to disagree about the appropriateness of log-transforming income in the first place (for instance, I have heard at least one highly influential researcher describe the transformation itself as a “sleight of hand”).

sentences.en: Understanding the association between income and happiness has the potential to help us make decisions that support human well-being.
sentences.zh: 了解收入和幸福之间的关系有可能帮助我们做出有利于人类福祉的决策。

sentences.en: While the association between income and happiness is one of the most studied topics in the happiness literature, it is common even for researchers to disagree about what it means for happiness to vary with Log(income), and sometimes even to disagree about the appropriateness of log-transforming income in the first place (for instance, I have heard at least one highly influential researcher describe the transformation itself as a “sleight of hand”).
sentences.zh: 虽然收入和幸福感之间的关联是幸福文献中研究最多的主题之一，但研究人员对于幸福感随 Log（收入）变化意味着什么存在分歧也很常见，有时甚至一开始就对收入对数变换的适当性产生分歧（例如，我听说至少有一位极具影响力的研究人员将变换本身描述为“花招”）。

blocks.html_en: These results suggest that the association between income and happiness is extremely systematic. The aggregate association between happiness and income was characterized almost perfectly as a linear association with Log(income), with group-level r’s = 0.98-0.99 across a range of happiness measures. This shows remarkable agreement with Bernoulli’s formulation in 1738, albeit in a different sense than he intended. While the linear relationship to Log(income) predicts an exponential decline in the marginal value of a dollar, it also predicts that proportional differences, such as a 10% increase, would be associated with the same difference in happiness regardless of income level.

sentences.en: These results suggest that the association between income and happiness is extremely systematic.
sentences.zh: 这些结果表明收入和幸福之间的关联是极其系统的。

sentences.en: The aggregate association between happiness and income was characterized almost perfectly as a linear association with Log(income), with group-level r’s = 0.98-0.99 across a range of happiness measures.
sentences.zh: 幸福与收入之间的总体关联几乎完美地描述为与 Log(收入) 的线性关联，在一系列幸福衡量指标中，群体层面的 r = 0.98-0.99。

sentences.en: This shows remarkable agreement with Bernoulli’s formulation in 1738, albeit in a different sense than he intended.
sentences.zh: 这表明与伯努利 1738 年的表述非常一致，尽管与他的意图不同。

sentences.en: While the linear relationship to Log(income) predicts an exponential decline in the marginal value of a dollar, it also predicts that proportional differences, such as a 10% increase, would be associated with the same difference in happiness regardless of income level.
sentences.zh: 虽然与 Log（收入）的线性关系预测一美元的边际价值呈指数下降，但它也预测比例差异（例如 10% 的增长）将与幸福感的相同差异相关，无论收入水平如何。

blocks.html_en: One might assume that the exponential decline in the marginal value of money means that most variation in happiness occurs in the lower income range. One might also assume that income effectively stops mattering in practical terms for happiness beyond some modest income threshold, even if the slope with Log(income) technically remains positive.

sentences.en: One might assume that the exponential decline in the marginal value of money means that most variation in happiness occurs in the lower income range.
sentences.zh: 人们可能会认为，货币边际价值的指数下降意味着幸福感的大部分变化发生在较低收入范围内。

sentences.en: One might also assume that income effectively stops mattering in practical terms for happiness beyond some modest income threshold, even if the slope with Log(income) technically remains positive.
sentences.zh: 人们还可能认为，超出一定的适度收入阈值后，收入实际上对幸福感不再重要，即使 Log（收入）的斜率在技术上仍然为正。

blocks.html_en: This report suggests that both of these assumptions are wrong in an important sense. The sizes of incomes increased exponentially across the income distribution, to such a degree that the association between happiness and a person’s position in the income distribution was highly linear (r = 0.96-0.99), even without a logarithmic transformation. In the end, traversing the space of possible incomes (e.g., by choosing one occupation over another, or one job or another) is predicted to result in non-diminishing differences in happiness, on average, across a wide range of income levels.

sentences.en: This report suggests that both of these assumptions are wrong in an important sense.
sentences.zh: 这份报告表明，这两种假设在重要意义上都是错误的。

sentences.en: The sizes of incomes increased exponentially across the income distribution, to such a degree that the association between happiness and a person’s position in the income distribution was highly linear (r = 0.96-0.99), even without a logarithmic transformation.
sentences.zh: 在收入分配中，收入规模呈指数级增长，以至于即使没有进行对数变换，幸福感与一个人在收入分配中的地位之间的关联也呈高度线性关系（r = 0.96-0.99）。

sentences.en: In the end, traversing the space of possible incomes (e.g., by choosing one occupation over another, or one job or another) is predicted to result in non-diminishing differences in happiness, on average, across a wide range of income levels.
sentences.zh: 最后，穿越可能收入的空间（例如，通过选择一种职业而不是另一种职业，或者选择一份工作或另一份工作）预计会导致不同收入水平的平均幸福感差异不会缩小。

blocks.html_en: On the other hand, in the domains where decisions can entail trade-offs between people with highly unequal incomes, such as philanthropy, tax policy, compensation decisions, and others, the exponentially diminishing value of dollars for happiness does predict exponential consequences. For example, a person would be predicted to generate astronomical returns for collective happiness if they could effectively invest a fraction of their income to improve the lives of people who are very poor. An employer could do the same by giving discretionary bonuses to the lowest paid workers instead of giving them only to the highest earners. Likewise, if gains from economic growth are shared throughout the income distribution, rather than mainly benefiting the well-off, it might greatly amplify the benefit of economic growth for a society’s well-being. Finally, high-stakes financial risks also yield exponential predictions, even at an individual level.

sentences.en: On the other hand, in the domains where decisions can entail trade-offs between people with highly unequal incomes, such as philanthropy, tax policy, compensation decisions, and others, the exponentially diminishing value of dollars for happiness does predict exponential consequences.
sentences.zh: 另一方面，在决策可能需要收入高度不平等的人们之间进行权衡的领域，例如慈善事业、税收政策、薪酬决策等，美元对幸福的价值呈指数级递减确实预示着指数级后果。

sentences.en: For example, a person would be predicted to generate astronomical returns for collective happiness if they could effectively invest a fraction of their income to improve the lives of people who are very poor.
sentences.zh: 例如，如果一个人能够有效地将其收入的一小部分用于改善贫困人口的生活，那么他将有望为集体幸福带来天文数字的回报。

sentences.en: An employer could do the same by giving discretionary bonuses to the lowest paid workers instead of giving them only to the highest earners.
sentences.zh: 雇主也可以采取同样的做法，向收入最低的工人发放酌情奖金，而不是只向收入最高的工人发放。

sentences.en: Likewise, if gains from economic growth are shared throughout the income distribution, rather than mainly benefiting the well-off, it might greatly amplify the benefit of economic growth for a society’s well-being.
sentences.zh: 同样，如果经济增长的收益在整个收入分配中分享，而不是主要让富人受益，那么它可能会大大放大经济增长对社会福祉的好处。

sentences.en: Finally, high-stakes financial risks also yield exponential predictions, even at an individual level.
sentences.zh: 最后，高风险的金融风险也会产生指数级的预测，即使是在个人层面也是如此。

blocks.html_en: Knowing when to think linearly and when to think exponentially is crucial for understanding the relationship between money and happiness.

sentences.en: Knowing when to think linearly and when to think exponentially is crucial for understanding the relationship between money and happiness.
sentences.zh: 知道何时进行线性思考以及何时进行指数思考对于理解金钱与幸福之间的关系至关重要。

blocks.html_en: Footnotes

sentences.en: Footnotes

blocks.html_en: 1. It’s worth noting that the highly linear shape is revealed when averaging across people, but of course that doesn’t tell us what the shape would be for a specific person. The linear shape is a sensible starting point for individual predictions, but a given individual could ultimately follow a somewhat different shape in reality. We just know that the cross-sectional average of those shapes is extremely linear.

sentences.en: It’s worth noting that the highly linear shape is revealed when averaging across people, but of course that doesn’t tell us what the shape would be for a specific person.
sentences.zh: 值得注意的是，在对人进行平均时会显示出高度线性的形状，但这当然并不能告诉我们特定人的形状是什么。

sentences.en: The linear shape is a sensible starting point for individual predictions, but a given individual could ultimately follow a somewhat different shape in reality.
sentences.zh: 线性形状是个人预测的合理起点，但给定的个人最终可能会遵循现实中略有不同的形状。

sentences.en: We just know that the cross-sectional average of those shapes is extremely linear.
sentences.zh: 我们只知道这些形状的横截面平均值是极其线性的。

blocks.html_en: Additionally, the highly linear shape reported in this paper describes the association between Log(income) and happiness, but can’t directly reveal the shape of the causal relationship. There is strong evidence that having more money does indeed cause higher happiness, as noted in the introduction, and given the strikingly linear shape of the association it is quite plausible that the causal relationship is also linear, but the field currently lacks definitive data on the nuanced shape of the causal relationship itself. Some of the implications discussed in this paper address the question: if the causal relationship follows the same shape as the cross-sectional association, what would the implications be? Further research would be needed to test this proposition explicitly.↩

sentences.en: There is strong evidence that having more money does indeed cause higher happiness, as noted in the introduction, and given the strikingly linear shape of the association it is quite plausible that the causal relationship is also linear, but the field currently lacks definitive data on the nuanced shape of the causal relationship itself.

blocks.html_en: 2. The same is true in reverse for exponential growth. Consider a hypothetical bacterial colony with unlimited space and food that doubles in size every 6 hours. No matter how long the growth period, if the number of bacteria is plotted on a linear axis, it will seem as if nothing happens until the last few days. But there is nothing special about a given number of days, and it takes a logarithmic axis to reveal the true pattern of growth.↩

sentences.zh: 指数增长的反面也是如此。

sentences.zh: 无论生长期有多长，如果将细菌数量绘制在线性轴上，直到最后几天似乎什么也没有发生。

blocks.html_en: 3. While this explanation interprets the result as if Log(income) is the key predictor, one might alternatively wonder whether it is relative income (i.e., income rank) that is actually the key predictor. It is likely that both relative and absolute income matter, but relative income seems unlikely to explain all or even most of the relationship between money and happiness. There are a few reasons for this, but a prominent one relates to a nation’s happiness and its association with income (5, 10). If happiness were determined entirely by one’s income relative to those in physical or psychological proximity, then we would have reason to expect a relatively steep income:happiness gradient within-nations (or within a given person’s reference group) and, by comparison, a relatively weak gradient between-nations (or between different people’s reference groups). In reality, not only is there a substantial income:happiness association between nations, but the slopes of happiness:Log(income) within-nations and between-nations are virtually identical in magnitude (e.g., see Figs. 11-12 in (10)). While well-being may be sensitive to relative income to some degree, the aforementioned evidence is consistent with a large role for absolute income and a limited role (or perhaps no net role at all) for relative income. At the same time, there is no straightforward and definitive answer for why money and happiness are related, and the forces underlying the relationship are likely to be complex, even if the average trend is simple.↩

sentences.en: While this explanation interprets the result as if Log(income) is the key predictor, one might alternatively wonder whether it is relative income (i.e., income rank) that is actually the key predictor.
sentences.zh: 虽然这种解释将结果解释为 Log(收入) 是关键预测变量，但人们可能想知道相对收入（即收入排名）是否实际上是关键预测变量。

sentences.en: In reality, not only is there a substantial income:happiness association between nations, but the slopes of happiness:Log(income) within-nations and between-nations are virtually identical in magnitude (e.g., see Figs.

sentences.zh: 虽然幸福感可能在某种程度上对相对收入敏感，但上述证据与绝对收入的巨大作用和相对收入的有限作用（或者可能根本没有净作用）是一致的。

blocks.html_en: 4. Technically, the ROI would be 9,900% once we subtract the loss incurred by the donor.↩

sentences.en: Technically, the ROI would be 9,900% once we subtract the loss incurred by the donor.↩
sentences.zh: 从技术上讲，扣除捐赠者造成的损失后，投资回报率为 9,900%。↩

blocks.html_en: 5. Why is the ROI ≈ 90,000,000%? Because 136,986 people would see their incomes double, in exchange for a 10% reduction for one person. Since the logarithmic math predicts that each beneficiary gains 6.58 times as much well-being as the single donor loses (log(2) / log(0.9)), we can multiply the 6.58 benefit:cost ratio by the 136,986 beneficiary:donor ratio to arrive at an approximate ROI estimate.↩

sentences.en: Why is the ROI ≈ 90,000,000%?
Because 136,986 people would see their incomes double, in exchange for a 10% reduction for one person.
sentences.zh: 因为 136,986 人的收入将增加一倍，而换取一个人收入减少 10%。

sentences.en: Since the logarithmic math predicts that each beneficiary gains 6.58 times as much well-being as the single donor loses (log(2) / log(0.9)), we can multiply the 6.58 benefit:cost ratio by the 136,986 beneficiary:donor ratio to arrive at an approximate ROI estimate.↩
sentences.zh: 由于对数数学预测每个受益人获得的福祉是单个捐赠者损失的 6.58 倍 (log(2) / log(0.9))，因此我们可以将 6.58 的收益：成本比率乘以 136,986 的受益人：捐赠者比率来得出近似的 ROI 估计值。↩

blocks.html_en: The income distribution

blocks.html_en: Fig. S1 The distribution of Log(income) across 20 income quantiles. Note that the x-axis is income quantile, and the y-axis is Log-scaled income. While the underlying shape is not perfectly linear, a linear fit can nevertheless explain it quite well (r = 0.98).

sentences.en: Fig. S1 The distribution of Log(income) across 20 income quantiles.
sentences.zh: 图 S1 Log（收入）在 20 个收入分位数中的分布。

sentences.en: Note that the x-axis is income quantile, and the y-axis is Log-scaled income.
sentences.zh: 请注意，x 轴是收入分位数，y 轴是对数尺度收入。

sentences.en: While the underlying shape is not perfectly linear, a linear fit can nevertheless explain it quite well (r = 0.98).
sentences.zh: 虽然基础形状并非完美线性，但线性拟合仍然可以很好地解释它 (r = 0.98)。

blocks.html_en: Note that while the correlation in Fig. S1 is very high (r = 0.98), the curvature in the tails is consistent with a normal distribution of income in logarithmic/exponential space, rather than a precisely linear relationship (i.e., a uniform distribution). The relevant point for this paper is that the distribution is approximately symmetrical, and increases just as quickly on the high end as the low end. But Log(income), and potentially happiness, do show faster change in the tails, especially the top and bottom 5%.

sentences.en: Note that while the correlation in Fig. S1 is very high (r = 0.98), the curvature in the tails is consistent with a normal distribution of income in logarithmic/exponential space, rather than a precisely linear relationship (i.e., a uniform distribution).
sentences.zh: 请注意，虽然图 S1 中的相关性非常高（r = 0.98），但尾部的曲率与对数/指数空间中的收入正态分布一致，而不是精确的线性关系（即均匀分布）。

sentences.en: The relevant point for this paper is that the distribution is approximately symmetrical, and increases just as quickly on the high end as the low end.
sentences.zh: 本文的相关观点是，分布近似对称，并且高端和低端的增长速度一样快。

sentences.en: But Log(income), and potentially happiness, do show faster change in the tails, especially the top and bottom 5%.
sentences.zh: 但对数（收入）和潜在的幸福感确实显示出尾部变化更快，尤其是顶部和底部 5% 的人。

blocks.html_en: Fig. S2 Household income distribution according to the Congressional Budget Office (2018 data, before taxes and transfers) compared to the distribution of incomes in the current study across 20 quantiles (as also shown in Fig. S1). CBO data is available by quintile for the first four quintiles and then in smaller quantiles within the top quintile.
